key_insight: A pan-cancer foundation model using immune concepts can predict who will respond to immune checkpoint inhibitors across many cancers and treatments.
clinical_relevance: This could help match patients to immunotherapy and support earlier trial selection and stratification when standard biomarkers do not generalize well.
method: Trained a concept-bottleneck transformer on bulk tumor transcriptomes from 10,184 tumors across 33 cancer types.
Encoded expression into 44 biologically grounded immune concepts covering cell states, microenvironment interactions, and signaling pathways.
Compared against 22 baseline methods and generated personalized response maps for interpretability.
evidence_signal: In 16 independent clinical cohorts across seven cancers and six checkpoint inhibitors, the model improved accuracy by 8.5% and AUPRC by 15.7%, with responders showing longer overall survival.
watch_item: Performance was validated across retrospective cohorts, so prospective testing and real-world implementation are still needed before clinical use.
paper_title: Generalizable AI predicts immunotherapy outcomes across cancers and treatments
